initial_program:
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARCAGI transformation: copy the sidecells into a 33 cross
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% ------- dimensions ------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

% centre of the grid (the puzzle data are always 57)
topRow    := 0.
bottomRow := n_rows - 1.          % 4
leftCol   := 0.
rightCol  := n_cols - 1.          % 6
centerRow := (n_rows - 1) / 2.    % 2
centerCol := (n_cols - 1) / 2.    % 3

% ------- corners (fixed coordinates) ----------------------------
output(0,0,Color) :- input(topRow,    leftCol,   Color),   output_cell(0,0).
output(0,2,Color) :- input(topRow,    rightCol,  Color),   output_cell(0,2).
output(2,0,Color) :- input(bottomRow, leftCol,   Color),   output_cell(2,0).
output(2,2,Color) :- input(bottomRow, rightCol,  Color),   output_cell(2,2).

% ------- columns closest to the centre -------------------------
%   top   arm (row0)    nearest nonzero column  centreCol
topArmCol := #max{ C : input(topRow, C, Color), Color != 0, C <= centerCol }.

%   bottom arm (row2)   nearest nonzero column  centreCol
bottomArmCol := #max{ C : input(bottomRow, C, Color), Color != 0, C <= centerCol }.

%   left   arm (col0)  nearest nonzero row     centreRow
leftArmRow := #max{ R : input(R, leftCol, Color), Color != 0, R <= centerRow }.

%   right  arm (col6)  nearest nonzero row     centreRow
rightArmRow := #max{ R : input(R, rightCol, Color), Color != 0, R <= centerRow }.

% ------- sidecells (cross arms) -------------------------------
%   top   arm (0,1)
output(0,1,Color) :- output_cell(0,1), topArmCol != topLeftCol,
                      input(topRow, topArmCol, Color).

%   bottom arm (2,1)
output(2,1,Color) :- output_cell(2,1), bottomArmCol != bottomLeftCol,
                      bottomArmCol != bottomRightCol,
                      input(bottomRow, bottomArmCol, Color).

%   left  arm (1,0)
output(1,0,Color) :- output_cell(1,0), leftArmRow != topRow,
                      input(leftArmRow, leftCol, Color).

%   right arm (1,2)
output(1,2,Color) :- output_cell(1,2), rightArmRow != topRow,
                      input(rightArmRow, rightCol, Color).

% ------- centre cell (same as the top arm) --------------------
output(1,1,Color) :- output_cell(1,1), input(topRow, topArmCol, Color).

% ------- output cells must be exactly one colour ----------------
output_cell(R,C) :- R = 0..2, C = 0..2.

1 { output(R,C,Color) : Color } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARCAGI transformation: copy the sidecells into a 33 cross
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% ------- dimensions ------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

% centre of the grid (the puzzle data are always 57)
% topRow = 0.
% bottomRow = n_rows - 1.
% leftCol = 0.
% rightCol = n_cols - 1.
% centerRow = (n_rows - 1) / 2.
% centerCol = (n_cols - 1) / 2.


% ------- corners (fixed coordinates) ----------------------------
output(0,0,Color) :- input(topRow,    leftCol,   Color),   output_cell(0,0).
output(0,2,Color) :- input(topRow,    rightCol,  Color),   output_cell(0,2).
output(2,0,Color) :- input(bottomRow, leftCol,   Color),   output_cell(2,0).
output(2,2,Color) :- input(bottomRow, rightCol,  Color),   output_cell(2,2).

% ------- columns closest to the centre -------------------------
%   top   arm (row0)    nearest nonzero column  centreCol
topArmCol = #max{ C : input(topRow, C, Color), Color != 0, C <= centerCol }.


%   bottom arm (row2)   nearest nonzero column  centreCol
bottomArmCol = #max{ C : input(bottomRow, C, Color), Color != 0, C <= centerCol }.


%   left   arm (col0)  nearest nonzero row     centreRow
leftArmRow = #max{ R : input(R, leftCol, Color), Color != 0, R <= centerRow }.


%   right  arm (col6)  nearest nonzero row     centreRow
rightArmRow = #max{ R : input(R, rightCol, Color), Color != 0, R <= centerRow }.


% ------- sidecells (cross arms) -------------------------------
%   top   arm (0,1)
output(0,1,Color) :- output_cell(0,1), topArmCol != topLeftCol,
                      input(topRow, topArmCol, Color).

%   bottom arm (2,1)
output(2,1,Color) :- output_cell(2,1), bottomArmCol != bottomLeftCol,
                      bottomArmCol != bottomRightCol,
                      input(bottomRow, bottomArmCol, Color).

%   left  arm (1,0)
output(1,0,Color) :- output_cell(1,0), leftArmRow != topRow,
                      input(leftArmRow, leftCol, Color).

%   right arm (1,2)
output(1,2,Color) :- output_cell(1,2), rightArmRow != topRow,
                      input(rightArmRow, rightCol, Color).

% ------- centre cell (same as the top arm) --------------------
output(1,1,Color) :- output_cell(1,1), input(topRow, topArmCol, Color).

% ------- output cells must be exactly one colour ----------------
output_cell(R,C) :- R = 0..2, C = 0..2.

1 { output(R,C,Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).


#show output/3.

<block>:38:50-51: error: syntax error, unexpected ",", expecting } or ;

rounds.program_after:
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARCAGI transformation: copy the sidecells into a 33 cross
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% ------- dimensions ------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

% centre of the grid (the puzzle data are always 57)
% topRow = 0.
% bottomRow = n_rows - 1.
% leftCol = 0.
% rightCol = n_cols - 1.
% centerRow = (n_rows - 1) / 2.
% centerCol = (n_cols - 1) / 2.


% ------- corners (fixed coordinates) ----------------------------
output(0,0,Color) :- input(topRow,    leftCol,   Color),   output_cell(0,0).
output(0,2,Color) :- input(topRow,    rightCol,  Color),   output_cell(0,2).
output(2,0,Color) :- input(bottomRow, leftCol,   Color),   output_cell(2,0).
output(2,2,Color) :- input(bottomRow, rightCol,  Color),   output_cell(2,2).

% ------- columns closest to the centre -------------------------
%   top   arm (row0)    nearest nonzero column  centreCol
topArmCol = #max{ C : input(topRow, C, Color), Color != 0, C <= centerCol }.


%   bottom arm (row2)   nearest nonzero column  centreCol
bottomArmCol = #max{ C : input(bottomRow, C, Color), Color != 0, C <= centerCol }.


%   left   arm (col0)  nearest nonzero row     centreRow
leftArmRow = #max{ R : input(R, leftCol, Color), Color != 0, R <= centerRow }.


%   right  arm (col6)  nearest nonzero row     centreRow
rightArmRow = #max{ R : input(R, rightCol, Color), Color != 0, R <= centerRow }.


% ------- sidecells (cross arms) -------------------------------
%   top   arm (0,1)
output(0,1,Color) :- output_cell(0,1), topArmCol != topLeftCol,
                      input(topRow, topArmCol, Color).

%   bottom arm (2,1)
output(2,1,Color) :- output_cell(2,1), bottomArmCol != bottomLeftCol,
                      bottomArmCol != bottomRightCol,
                      input(bottomRow, bottomArmCol, Color).

%   left  arm (1,0)
output(1,0,Color) :- output_cell(1,0), leftArmRow != topRow,
                      input(leftArmRow, leftCol, Color).

%   right arm (1,2)
output(1,2,Color) :- output_cell(1,2), rightArmRow != topRow,
                      input(rightArmRow, rightCol, Color).

% ------- centre cell (same as the top arm) --------------------
output(1,1,Color) :- output_cell(1,1), input(topRow, topArmCol, Color).

% ------- output cells must be exactly one colour ----------------
output_cell(R,C) :- R = 0..2, C = 0..2.

1 { output(R,C,Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).


#show output/3.

final_program:
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARCAGI transformation: copy the sidecells into a 33 cross
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% ------- dimensions ------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

% centre of the grid (the puzzle data are always 57)
% topRow = 0.
% bottomRow = n_rows - 1.
% leftCol = 0.
% rightCol = n_cols - 1.
% centerRow = (n_rows - 1) / 2.
% centerCol = (n_cols - 1) / 2.


% ------- corners (fixed coordinates) ----------------------------
output(0,0,Color) :- input(topRow,    leftCol,   Color),   output_cell(0,0).
output(0,2,Color) :- input(topRow,    rightCol,  Color),   output_cell(0,2).
output(2,0,Color) :- input(bottomRow, leftCol,   Color),   output_cell(2,0).
output(2,2,Color) :- input(bottomRow, rightCol,  Color),   output_cell(2,2).

% ------- columns closest to the centre -------------------------
%   top   arm (row0)    nearest nonzero column  centreCol
topArmCol = #max{ C : input(topRow, C, Color), Color != 0, C <= centerCol }.


%   bottom arm (row2)   nearest nonzero column  centreCol
bottomArmCol = #max{ C : input(bottomRow, C, Color), Color != 0, C <= centerCol }.


%   left   arm (col0)  nearest nonzero row     centreRow
leftArmRow = #max{ R : input(R, leftCol, Color), Color != 0, R <= centerRow }.


%   right  arm (col6)  nearest nonzero row     centreRow
rightArmRow = #max{ R : input(R, rightCol, Color), Color != 0, R <= centerRow }.


% ------- sidecells (cross arms) -------------------------------
%   top   arm (0,1)
output(0,1,Color) :- output_cell(0,1), topArmCol != topLeftCol,
                      input(topRow, topArmCol, Color).

%   bottom arm (2,1)
output(2,1,Color) :- output_cell(2,1), bottomArmCol != bottomLeftCol,
                      bottomArmCol != bottomRightCol,
                      input(bottomRow, bottomArmCol, Color).

%   left  arm (1,0)
output(1,0,Color) :- output_cell(1,0), leftArmRow != topRow,
                      input(leftArmRow, leftCol, Color).

%   right arm (1,2)
output(1,2,Color) :- output_cell(1,2), rightArmRow != topRow,
                      input(rightArmRow, rightCol, Color).

% ------- centre cell (same as the top arm) --------------------
output(1,1,Color) :- output_cell(1,1), input(topRow, topArmCol, Color).

% ------- output cells must be exactly one colour ----------------
output_cell(R,C) :- R = 0..2, C = 0..2.

1 { output(R,C,Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).


#show output/3.
